test_cases.name: BYOD-001 | Happy Path — New Customer, Physical SIM
test_cases.custom_requirements:
CANONICAL TEMPLATE. AR: AR-5 | Type: Positive | Technique: EP (happy-path representative of valid class).

Feature: BYOD activation lets a store rep put a Metro subscriber onto the network using a phone the customer already owns. Rep enters the customer's handset IMEI; platform offers to register it as customer-owned, rep picks device type, validates SIM, assigns number and plan, completes sale. BYOD device priced $0 — only SIM, plan, taxes and accessories charged.

Related: RTPL-2061 (Epic), RTPL-2205, RTPL-2069, RTPL-2417.

Automation Notes: IMEI input auto-submits at 15 digits — do not click submit; await network idle. Generate IMEI in fixture (Luhn); assert by device-type value (BYOD-ANDROID), not i18n label. Discover MDN/plan at run time (first eligible) — never hard-code. MDN selection 5–10s latency; executeActivity timeout 130s — wait on activity completion, not HTTP 200. Payment cash/exact: collect-cash {amount: dueImmediately} then collect-payment; do not overpay. Shared fixture ByodActivationFlow — parameterize IMEI + SIM only.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Store rep logged in at COR store; new-account flow reached (identity validation skipped in test env). ConfigCat flag enable_subscriber_activation_byod = ON. Freshly generated random Luhn-valid IMEI (customer-owned; not in local inventory, not blacklisted).

Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid 15-digit generated per run (e.g. TAC 35283811 + random body + check digit), NOT a mock/seed IMEI. SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM matching ^89[a-zA-Z0-9]{17}F$ (probe live; on sim-card-active-or-used try next). All other values (device type, MDN, plan) discovered at run time — do not pin.

steps.action: Rep fills account details and clicks Continue (random customer via faker)
steps.expected: [BE] POST account-new/start -> state.businessData.account.accountNumber returned. [FE] city auto-fills UPPERCASE, state shows code; "ID verification" header appears

steps.action: Rep enters the phone number and clicks Continue
steps.expected: [FE] ID-verification fields prefilled from account, uppercased. [BE] POST validate-identity -> state.businessData.completion.eKey. [FE] success text "Congratulations, your customer's ID has been verified." + verified-id field = eKey

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue to IMEI validation
steps.expected: [FE] "IMEI validation" header visible. Cart panel: Account type "New account", Full name, Address, City/state/zip, Email, ID verified, ID verified ref# = eKey

steps.action: Rep enters the IMEI (fixture singleSims[])
steps.expected: [FE] input auto-submits at 15 digits. [BE] POST validate-device -> capture state.lines[0] + state.processId (teardown). [FE] "IMEI not found" drawer appears, non-dismissable

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue in the drawer
steps.expected: [Both] accept-add-as-byod dispatched; device-type picker appears; [BE] availableByodDeviceTypes = handset codes only (no BYOW-*, no BYOD-DSI)

steps.action: Rep selects "BYOP Android" (value BYOD-ANDROID)
steps.expected: [FE] "BYOP Android" selected; Continue enabled — flow does NOT advance yet

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue
steps.expected: [BE] deviceValidationDetails: selectedByodDeviceType='BYOD-ANDROID', isByod=true, deviceSource='B', basePrice=0. [FE] "SIM validation" header; pSIM card "IMEI 1 - PSIM" content = IMEI1; single-SIM -> 1 card, pSIM checked, SIM label "ICCID SIM *", input empty, Continue DISABLED. Cart "Device" row = $0.00

steps.action: Rep drills into the cart Device row
steps.expected: [FE] device details: Device type, Item ID=localItemSku, Description=pricing.device.description, IMEI=IMEI1, Price=basePrice, Tax=taxAmount, Final price=totalAfterTax

steps.action: Rep enters the physical SIM ICCID (fixture iccids[])
steps.expected: [FE] ICCID accepted; Continue ENABLED — flow does NOT advance yet

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue
steps.expected: [BE] POST validate-sim-card -> state.lines[0]; GET available-mdns -> MDN list. [FE] "Phone number selection" header; New/Port cards (New checked); zip prefilled. Cart "SIM card" row = SIM total, "Total due" updated. Each MDN = (npa) nxx-* submarketName, stateCode

steps.action: Rep selects a new number (first available MDN)
steps.expected: [FE] MDN radio selected — flow does NOT advance yet

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue
steps.expected: [BE] GET selection-options/protections -> protection plans. [FE] "Protection selection" header; card count = plans.length. Cart "Phone number" row = (npa) nxx-****

steps.action: Rep declines protection (Skip, confirm "Decline all protection options?")
steps.expected: [BE] configure-protection -> protectionConfigDetails.skipped=true; GET selection-options/plans. [FE] "Plan selection" header; cards = plans.length, index-0 preselected, no excluded flex codes, rate badges $x per month sorted high->low

steps.action: Rep selects the first eligible plan
steps.expected: [FE] plan card aria-label "Select" -> "Selected" — flow does NOT advance yet

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue
steps.expected: [BE] POST configure-plan -> planConfigDetails set, monthly > $0; GET selection-options/promotions. [FE] "Promotion selection" header. Cart "Plan (SOC)" row = $x / mo., Estimated monthly + Total due update

steps.action: Rep skips promotions (Skip)
steps.expected: [BE] configure-promotions; GET selection-options/addons. [FE] "Add on services" header with category tabs (International/Services/Data/Storage/Security/Others)

steps.action: Rep skips add-ons (Skip)
steps.expected: [Both] create-subscriber confirm dialog appears

steps.action: Rep confirms activation (Create subscriber)
steps.expected: [BE] Metro CreateByodDevice + activate-subscriber accepted with deviceInfoSource='BYOD-ANDROID' (not "DEVICE"); no error 100000004/999999963. [FE] dialog offers Continue to order summary (add-line / complete-order)

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue to order summary (single line)
steps.expected: [FE] "Order summary" header visible

steps.action: Order Summary — rep reviews
steps.expected: [Both] Order ID=state.orderId; Account number; Lines in order; Total MRC; Total taxes & fees; Edge due immediately; device $0.00, SIM $10 + tax (no discount)

steps.action: Add Accessory — rep opens Review invoice, adds none
steps.expected: [FE] "Review invoice" header; item rows = payment.lineItems.length + 3; Balance due = payment.totalAmount; totals unchanged with no accessory

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue to payment
steps.expected: [BE] POST prepare-payment. [FE] payment screen; amount to collect = due-immediately

steps.action: Rep pays cash, exact amount
steps.expected: [Both] collect-cash -> collect-payment succeeds. [BE] POST set-payment-amount/collect -> SaleRecord persists with real number (no TEMP-), saleRecordFailed=false; LINE ONLY ACTIVATION for phone, SIM line $10 + tax, no device charge line (assert via SQL / receipt)

test_cases.name: BYOD-002 | "IMEI not found" Drawer — Content and Both Exits
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (drawer copy needs data-testid) | Type: Positive + Negative | Technique: EP (one case per user decision branch).

Feature: When rep enters an IMEI Metro does not find in store inventory, RTGO shows a non-dismissable drawer saying device looks customer-owned, offering Continue (register as BYOD) or Back (retype IMEI). Entry gate to whole BYOD flow.

Related: RTPL-2076, RTPL-2069.

Automation Notes: Drawer component, NOT v-dialog — assert role/aria, not just visibility. Run Back as separate test — cancel-line is terminal for the line. Guard against legacy string "Confirm BYOD to add to inventory" appearing anywhere (old-path regression). Two runs: Run A (Continue), Run B (Back), each on fresh line.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Activation flow at device-validate step. ConfigCat flag enable_subscriber_activation_byod = ON.

Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid 15-digit customer-owned handset IMEI (not in local inventory).

steps.action: Rep enters the IMEI (random)
steps.expected: [FE] input auto-submits at 15 digits; "IMEI not found" drawer opens, non-dismissable (no click-outside close)

steps.action: Rep reads the drawer copy
steps.expected: [FE] title "IMEI not found"; body interpolates the entered IMEI; primary Continue + secondary Back present

steps.action: (Run A) Rep clicks Continue
steps.expected: [Both] accept-add-as-byod dispatched; [FE] device-type picker appears; [BE] reasonCodeForAction transitions device-not-in-inventory -> select-byod-device-type

steps.action: (Run B, fresh line) Rep clicks Back
steps.expected: [Both] cancel-line dispatched; [FE] rep returned to IMEI field; [BE] line cancelled, no CreateByodDevice call made

test_cases.name: BYOD-003 | Device Type Picker — Correct Options for Handset
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (radios need data-testid) | Type: Positive + Negative | Technique: EP (option-set equivalence per device class).

Feature: After rep accepts device as customer-owned, RTGO asks what KIND of device it is. Options must match the device class the IMEI resolves to — handset offers handset types only, never watch types; disallowed DSI variant must never appear.

Related: RTPL-2420 (BYOD-DSI must not appear), RTPL-2205, RTPL-2557.

Automation Notes: Assert by radio value (code), not i18n label. Two-layer check: intercept availableByodDeviceTypes API field AND assert rendered DOM — catches BE and FE regressions independently.

Preconditions / Test Data:
BYOD flow entered via accept-add-as-byod on a handset IMEI.

Test Data (FE): Handset IMEI = random Luhn-valid 15-digit handset IMEI (customer-owned).

steps.action: Rep reaches the device-type picker
steps.expected: [FE] required radio group shown

steps.action: Rep reads the offered options
steps.expected: [Both] rendered options = "BYOP Android" (BYOD-ANDROID), "BYOP iPhone" (BYOD-IPHONE), "Tablet" (BYOT); [BE] availableByodDeviceTypes contains no BYOW-* codes

steps.action: Rep confirms DSI is not offered
steps.expected: [FE] BYOD-DSI not rendered (FE HIDDEN_DEVICE_TYPES) even if the API returns it

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue with nothing selected
steps.expected: [FE] required-field validation blocks; flow does NOT advance

test_cases.name: BYOD-004 | Device Type Persistence + Forwarded to Metro
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (BYOT variant needs primary line + tablet plan data) | Type: Positive | Technique: State transition (assert state invariant across every activity edge).

Feature: Once rep picks BYOD device type, choice must stay attached to the line through every later step and be sent to Metro at activation. Early defects lost it (line reverted to Unknown) or hardcoded "DEVICE" which Metro rejected. Proves type survives end-to-end per device class.

Related: RTPL-2403 (device type lost), RTPL-2417 (hardcoded "DEVICE").

Automation Notes: Snapshot with GET sales/customers/process/lines/{processId} between activities — assert API state, not UI. RTPL-2403 regression: full-text search of line state for selected code must return >=1 match after selection. BYOT run inherits tablet plans-null bug risk — gate/skip until fixed.

Preconditions / Test Data:
BYOD flow with device-type picker reached. For BYOT run: primary phone line already added first (rule 9) — tablet cannot be line 1.

Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid per run with TAC matching device class under test: Android -> BYOD-ANDROID, iPhone -> BYOD-IPHONE, Tablet -> BYOT. SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM matching ^89[a-zA-Z0-9]{17}F$. Data-driven over three device classes; plan/MDN discovered at run time.

steps.action: Rep selects the device type under test (e.g. BYOD-ANDROID)
steps.expected: [FE] option selected; Continue enabled — flow does NOT advance yet

steps.action: Rep clicks Continue
steps.expected: [BE] selectedByodDeviceType = chosen code, non-null; [FE] flow advances to SIM step

steps.action: Rep advances through SIM -> MDN -> protection(skip) -> plan -> promotions(skip) -> add-ons(skip) as in BYOD-001 (first eligible values)
steps.expected: [BE] GET line state after EACH activity: selectedByodDeviceType unchanged, never null, never Unknown

steps.action: Rep confirms activation
steps.expected: [BE] Metro CreateByodDevice + activate-subscriber carry the selected code (deviceInfoSource valid, not "DEVICE"); no error 100000004 / 999999963

steps.action: Rep completes Order Summary -> Add Accessory -> cash payment (exact)
steps.expected: [BE] processCompleted=true, saleRecordNumber populated

steps.action: Repeat runs for BYOD-IPHONE and BYOT (BYOT as line 2)
steps.expected: [BE] same invariants per device class

test_cases.name: BYOD-005 | Cross-Device Guard — Watch Code Rejected on Handset
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-5 (passed live 2026-07-09) | Type: Negative | Technique: EP invalid class + API-level bypass test.

Feature: Device-type picker must be trustworthy end-to-end: a watch-only code (BYOW-SAM) must never be accepted for a handset IMEI. Backend must reject mismatched code rather than silently flipping line into a watch — past bug returned 200 OK and overwrote isWatch false->true, corrupting activation.

Related: RTPL-2556.

Automation Notes: Two-layer test: FE absence + API rejection. API layer is the real guard (RTPL-2556 was 200 OK + isWatch flip). Confirmed live 2026-07-09: 400 invalid-byod-device-type, isWatch preserved. Align exact code with current backend if it changes.

Preconditions / Test Data:
BYOD flow: handset IMEI validated and accepted as BYOD (isWatch=false).

Test Data (FE): Handset IMEI = random Luhn-valid 15-digit (customer-owned). Invalid device-type code (API bypass) = BYOW-SAM (watch-only).

steps.action: Rep validates the handset IMEI and accepts BYOD
steps.expected: [BE] isWatch=false

steps.action: Rep views the picker
steps.expected: [FE] BYOW-SAM is not offered / not selectable

steps.action: (API bypass) Submit select-byod-device-type with deviceType:"BYOW-SAM" via direct API call
steps.expected: [BE] HTTP 400 invalid-byod-device-type — "Selected BYOD device type 'BYOW-SAM' is not available."

steps.action: Re-read line state
steps.expected: [BE] isWatch still false (NOT overwritten); selectedByodDeviceType unchanged

test_cases.name: BYOD-006 | IMEI2 Prompt — DF/DS Conditional Gating
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (needs provisioned DF/DS + non-DF/DS IMEIs) | Type: Positive + Negative | Technique: Decision table (deviceProperty x isMissingImei).

Feature: Some customer-owned devices carry two IMEIs (dual-SIM). Metro flags via deviceProperty (DF/DS) or isMissingImei. RTGO must ask for second IMEI only for those devices, skip for everyone else — wrong behavior produces invalid AddByodDevice payloads Metro rejects.

Decision table: R1 deviceProperty starts DF -> prompt shown prefilled, payload includes imeiList. R2 starts DS -> shown prefilled, includes imeiList. R3 null/other + isMissingImei=true -> shown, includes imeiList. R4 null/other + false -> skipped, no imeiList.

Related: RTPL-2424, RTPL-2205 (gap #2).

Automation Notes: One automated test per decision-table rule; deviceProperty is Metro-driven — needs provisioned DF/DS and non-DF/DS IMEIs, cannot be forced by random IMEI (AR-4). Verify imeiList presence on outbound proxy call (backend log/mock), not just UI. Open question (RTPL-2424): imeiList shape — [IMEI2] succeeded, [IMEI1, IMEI2] rejected; assert current contract before pinning.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): R4 IMEI (no prompt) = non-DF/DS BYOD IMEI (Metro returns deviceProperty:null, isMissingImei:false), e.g. 352199223983499. R1/R2 IMEI (prompt) = dual-IMEI (DF/DS) BYOD IMEI, e.g. 350180500000001. IMEI2 (when prompted) = valid 15-digit, different from primary.

steps.action: (R4) Rep runs BYOD flow with a non-DF/DS IMEI and selects the device type
steps.expected: [FE] flow advances straight to SIM step — NO IMEI2 prompt; [BE] no provide-imei2-for-byod reason code

steps.action: (R4) Rep completes activation (first eligible values)
steps.expected: [BE] AddByodDevice called WITHOUT imeiList; no Metro error 990000101

steps.action: (R1) Rep runs BYOD flow with a dual-IMEI (DF/DS) device and selects the device type
steps.expected: [Both] IMEI2 step shown, prefilled from Metro response where available; [BE] reason code provide-imei2-for-byod

steps.action: (R1) Rep enters a valid 15-digit IMEI2 != primary and Continues
steps.expected: [Both] accepted; flow advances

steps.action: (R1) Rep enters a non-15-digit IMEI2 (14 digits)
steps.expected: [FE] field validation rejects (exact: 15); flow does NOT advance

test_cases.name: BYOD-007 | IMEI2 Duplicate of Primary — Fail Fast
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (needs provisioned dual-IMEI device; re-run — inconclusive live) | Type: Negative | Technique: EP invalid class (duplicate identity).

Feature: On dual-IMEI device rep enters second IMEI. If they mistype the primary IMEI into the second field, RTGO must reject right there at the IMEI2 step — not accept (200 OK) and blow up five steps later at configure-protection with raw "Metro proxy error" (original defect). Failing fast keeps error recoverable.

Related: RTPL-2418.

Automation Notes: Key regression signal is WHERE the error fires (at entry, not later) — assert the step, not only the message. Verify exact message string (used for localization and rep escalation).

Preconditions / Test Data:
BYOD flow at IMEI2 prompt (dual-IMEI device — see BYOD-006 R1).

Test Data (FE): Primary IMEI = dual-IMEI (DF/DS) BYOD IMEI, e.g. 352838114032219. IMEI2 (invalid) = same value as primary. IMEI2 (valid, recovery) = different valid 15-digit IMEI.

steps.action: Rep reaches the IMEI2 prompt (dual-IMEI device)
steps.expected: [FE] IMEI2 field shown

steps.action: Rep enters IMEI2 = primary IMEI and Continues
steps.expected: [BE] HTTP 400 immediately, code BYOD_DUPLICATE_IMEI, anchored to imei2, message "This IMEI is already used as the primary device on this line. Enter a different IMEI."

steps.action: Rep observes the flow did not advance
steps.expected: [FE] still on collect-device-info (no 200-OK swallow); [BE] no late failure 999999963 at configure-protection

steps.action: Rep corrects IMEI2 to a different value and Continues
steps.expected: [Both] accepted; flow advances

test_cases.name: BYOD-008 | eSIM EID Entry — Happy Path + Length Boundaries
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-2 (EID write-back mock QA-rejected; no usable EID test data) | Type: Positive + Edge | Technique: BVA on EID length (31/32/33), EP on charset.

Feature: eSIM BYOD device has no physical SIM — rep types the device's 32-digit EID so Metro can provision embedded SIM. RTGO must accept valid 32-digit EID, reject wrong lengths/charsets, complete activation with no physical SIM line charged.

Related: RTPL-2634 (EID write-back mock), RTPL-2414 (missing-eid), RTPL-2205 (gap #5: AddBYODESIm).

Automation Notes: BVA as one parameterized spec: [31, 32, 33, 32+alpha]. eid-immutable reason code exists — changing EID after acceptance is candidate follow-up case. Blocked today: RTPL-2634 mock QA-rejected AND workbook EID column float-corrupted — provision usable EIDs to raise above AR-2.

Preconditions / Test Data:
BYOD flow with eSIM-capable device; EID collection screen reached (missing-eid / eid-required-for-add-device). Environment with EID write-back available (real IBillingInventoryManagement has no mock — RTPL-2634).

Test Data (FE): IMEI = eSIM-capable BYOD IMEI (e.g. UAT 353177128173520). EID (valid) = 32-digit numeric ^[0-9]{32}$ (e.g. 89049032000000000000000000000001). EID (invalid, BVA) = 31 digits; 33 digits; 32 chars incl. a letter.

steps.action: Rep reaches the EID screen (eSIM IMEI)
steps.expected: [FE] title "Add to inventory: EID", field label "EID", placeholder "32-digits"

steps.action: Rep enters a 31-digit EID and Continues
steps.expected: [FE] rejected (exact 32); flow does NOT advance

steps.action: Rep enters a 33-digit EID and Continues
steps.expected: [FE] rejected

steps.action: Rep enters 32 chars incl. a letter and Continues
steps.expected: [FE] rejected by ^[0-9]{32}$

steps.action: Rep enters a valid 32-digit EID and Continues
steps.expected: [Both] provide-eid accepted; deviceValidationDetails.eid set; simOptions[0].simType='ESIM'; flow advances

steps.action: Rep completes plan -> promotions(skip) -> add-ons(skip) -> activate -> Order Summary -> Add Accessory -> cash payment
steps.expected: [BE] eSIM provisioned (AddBYODESIm after CreateByodDevice); NO physical SIM line in SaleRecord (RTPL-2578); SaleRecord persists (real number)

test_cases.name: BYOD-009 | eSIM Missing EID — Reason Code and Block
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-3 (needs eSIM BYOD IMEI provisioned) | Type: Negative | Technique: EP invalid class (absent mandatory input).

Feature: When eSIM device needs an EID, backend must tell frontend WHY it is pausing (reason code missing-eid) so FE can render EID entry panel. Past bug returned correct actions but null reason code — blank screen, no way forward. Empty/partial EID must never advance.

Related: RTPL-2414.

Automation Notes: Assert reason code in API response, not just UI error — RTPL-2414 bug was null reason code with correct actions. Cover BOTH triggers: missing-eid and eid-required-for-add-device (via EDGE-add IMEI, e.g. 350180600000001) — same widget, different reason code.

Preconditions / Test Data:
BYOD eSIM flow at EID collection.

Test Data (FE): IMEI = eSIM BYOD IMEI that needs an EID (e.g. 353177128173520). EID (invalid inputs) = blank; 12-digit partial.

steps.action: Rep reaches the EID step for the eSIM device
steps.expected: [BE] availableActions:["provide-eid"] AND reasonCodeForAction:"missing-eid" (NOT null); [FE] EID panel renders with title + field (not a blank step)

steps.action: Rep leaves the EID blank and clicks Continue
steps.expected: [FE] required-field validation blocks; no API submission; flow does NOT advance

steps.action: Rep enters a 12-digit partial EID and clicks Continue
steps.expected: [Both] rejected; [BE] still at collect-device-info, activity pending, no activation submitted

test_cases.name: BYOD-010 | Customer-Owned Confirmation -> BYOD Classification (Not Dealer)
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (needs Metro-recognized customer-owned catalog IMEI in UAT3) | Type: Positive + Negative | Technique: Decision table (recognized-in-catalog x in-inventory x accept/decline).

Feature: Some customer-owned phones ARE in Metro's device catalog though not in store's own inventory. When rep scans one, RTGO shows "customer-owned device" confirmation. Accepting must classify line as BYOD — device $0, physical SIM $10 + tax — NOT dealer stock (which would wrongly give free SIM and dealer device price). Revenue-correctness case.

Related: RTPL-2737 (classified Dealer instead of BYOD), RTPL-2674, RTPL-2578.

Automation Notes: Assert InventoryType/deviceSource from API state AFTER classification block re-runs — asserting right after accept click can pass while final state is wrong (RTPL-2737 root cause). Regression controls: store-stock and reactivation devices keep own pricing; customer-owned WATCH stays BYOD. en + es dialog localization (RTPL-2674 AC). Two runs: Run A (Accept) full flow, Run B (Decline).

Preconditions / Test Data:
New activation; IMEI recognized by Metro catalog but NOT in local store inventory; not port-in; not reactivation.

Test Data (FE): IMEI = Metro-recognized catalog model the store does NOT stock (customer-owned confirmation IMEI). SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM matching ^89[a-zA-Z0-9]{17}F$.

steps.action: Rep enters the customer-owned IMEI
steps.expected: [Both] confirmation dialog shown; [BE] reasonCodeForAction:"customer-owned-device-confirmation", activityStatus:waiting-for-action

steps.action: (Run A) Rep clicks Accept Customer Owned
steps.expected: [BE] device re-validated; after classification block InventoryType=Byod (NOT Dealer), deviceSource='B'

steps.action: Rep enters the physical SIM and Continues
steps.expected: [Both] SIM validated; [BE] device $0.00, SIM $10 + tax, no discount (applyActivationDiscount=false)

steps.action: Rep completes MDN -> protection(skip) -> plan -> promotions(skip) -> add-ons(skip) -> activate -> Order Summary -> Add Accessory -> cash payment (first eligible values)
steps.expected: [Both] Order Summary shows device $0, SIM $10 + tax; [BE] SaleRecord persists with BYOD device $0 line (no dealer price, no free SIM)

steps.action: (Run B, fresh line) Rep clicks Decline
steps.expected: [Both] line cancelled; no activation

test_cases.name: BYOD-011 | SIM Pricing Parity Matrix
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-5 (R2 passed live 2026-07-09) | Type: Positive | Technique: Decision table (device source x SIM type), legacy-parity oracle.

Feature: SIM charge depends on device source and SIM type; RTGO must match legacy RT POS exactly. BYOD physical SIM = $10 + tax no discount; A-Stock physical SIM = free ($10, discounted -$10); reactivation and eSIM = no SIM line at all. Wrong = over/under-charges every sale.

Decision table (canonical, legacy addtoinvoice.prg): R1 A-Stock PSIM $10 / -$10 / tax $0. R2 BYOD PSIM $10 / none / tax on $10. R3 Reactivation any -> no SIM line. R4 Any flow eSIM -> no SIM line.

Related: RTPL-2578 (Verified; canonical rules).

Automation Notes: One test per rule; R2 is BYOD-critical guard (also protects SIM-priced-before-coupon ordering — RTPL-2626 root cause). Assert cart, Order Summary, AND SaleRecord — bug lived in divergence between them. SIM tax always on selling price, even in Tax-Before-Discount stores. R2 confirmed live 2026-07-09: SIM $10 + $1 tax, no discount.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI per rule — random Luhn BYOD handset (R2), catalog A-Stock IMEI (R1), reactivation IMEI (R3), eSIM BYOD IMEI (R4). SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM (R1/R2); EID for R4. One run per rule; each carried to cash payment so both pricing sites asserted.

steps.action: (R2) Rep runs a BYOD pSIM activation to Order Summary
steps.expected: [Both] SIM $10, no discount, tax on $10; device $0

steps.action: (R2) Rep pays cash and completes
steps.expected: [BE] child payment SaleRecord repeats same SIM $10 + tax (payment-time repricing site consistent — was hardcoded)

steps.action: (R4) Rep runs a BYOD eSIM activation
steps.expected: [Both] NO SIM line anywhere — cart, Order Summary, SaleRecord

steps.action: (R1 control) Rep runs an A-Stock pSIM activation
steps.expected: [Both] SIM 10 / -10 / tax 0 unchanged

test_cases.name: BYOD-012 | Free SIM Coupon COUP_NEUFREESIM — SIM Repriced to $0
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-1 (BLOCKED: COUP_NEUFREESIM not present in UAT3 catalog) | Type: Positive | Technique: EP + state transition (coupon applied AFTER SIM priced).

Feature: Promotion can make SIM free on BYOD sale. Rep selects free-SIM coupon at promotions step — AFTER SIM already priced $10. RTGO must re-price SIM to $0 (tax $0) when coupon applied. Ordering is the whole point: early bug priced SIM before coupon existed and never re-priced — coupon had no effect.

Related: RTPL-2626 (Verified), RTPL-2578.

Automation Notes: Coupon is REP-SELECTED at promotions step — never auto-applied. Data-drive both coupon codes (COUP_NEUFREESIM, UPGFMS). Blocked today: UAT3 returned eligiblePromotions:[] for BYOD line — coupon must be seeded to raise above AR-1.

Preconditions / Test Data:
BYOD activation with physical SIM already validated ($10 at SIM step); coupon selected later at promotions.

Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI. SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM matching ^89[a-zA-Z0-9]{17}F$. Coupon (selected at promotions) = COUP_NEUFREESIM (also cover UPGFMS).

steps.action: Rep validates the physical SIM
steps.expected: [Both] SIM priced $10 + tax at the SIM step

steps.action: Rep reaches promotions and selects the free-SIM coupon (COUP_NEUFREESIM)
steps.expected: [Both] coupon accepted; cart shows SIM $0 + discount

steps.action: Rep continues to Order Summary
steps.expected: [Both] SIM line $0.00, tax $0.00; total reflects the discount

steps.action: Rep completes Add Accessory -> cash payment (exact)
steps.expected: [BE] SaleRecord SIM line price 0 / tax 0; coupon match works with/without COUP_ prefix

steps.action: (Control) Rep runs the same BYOD flow without the coupon
steps.expected: [Both] SIM stays $10 + tax (BYOD-011 R2)

test_cases.name: BYOD-013 | Long Coupon Code — SaleRecord Persists (No TEMP- Stuck Sale)
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-1 (BLOCKED: >15-char coupon must be seeded in UAT3) | Type: Edge | Technique: BVA on persisted coupon length (varchar(15) boundary).

Feature: DB column storing applied coupon (EdgeCoupon) is varchar(15). If coupon code (with COUP_ prefix) longer than 15 chars, saving sale throws SQL truncation — subscriber activated and paid but SaleRecord never persists, stuck TEMP- sale retries forever, no receipt. RTGO must strip prefix so code fits; sale must save with real number.

Related: RTPL-2671 (QA in Progress), RTPL-2544 (same class, EID).

Automation Notes: Absence of TEMP- prefix in sale number = cheap decisive oracle. Old tests used COUP_TEST (9 chars) and never caught this — keep long coupon permanently in regression data. Blocked today: >15-char coupon must be seeded in UAT3. Also verify retries capped at 5 + Datadog alert (no infinite hourly retry).

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI. SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM. Coupon (overflow) = COUP_NEUFREESIMPOBYD (20 chars; 15 after strip). Coupon (control, short) = COUP_TEST (9 chars).

steps.action: Rep runs a BYOD activation, selects the overflow coupon, and pays cash
steps.expected: [BE] activation completes; SaleRecord gets a REAL number (no TEMP- prefix); no SqlException 8152 in logs

steps.action: Rep fetches the receipt (print + email)
steps.expected: [Both] both succeed; no sale-record-not-found

steps.action: Inspect the persisted sale
steps.expected: [BE] EdgeCoupon holds the STRIPPED code (no COUP_ prefix), fits varchar(15)

steps.action: Control: repeat with the short coupon
steps.expected: [Both] same success path

test_cases.name: BYOD-014 | BYOD-Only Plans Visibility (40L1WEB, BYOD10GB3)
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (backend passes live; FE blacklist bug gates UI half) | Type: Positive + Negative | Technique: EP (BYOD vs non-BYOD line) with negative assertion.

Feature: Two plans are BYOD-exclusive — 40L1WEB and BYOD10GB3. BYOD line must be offered them; A-Stock (non-BYOD) line must never see them. Wrong = hides BYOD pricing from reps or sells BYOD-only plan on wrong line.

Related: RTPL-2438 (Closed/Done), RTPL-2339 (temporary FE blacklist), RTPL-1911.

EXECUTION GATE: as of 2026-07-09 plan-configure.vue still ships RTPL-2339 PLAN_BLACKLIST_MAP hiding both plans from ALL flows in UI, though backend returns them correctly for BYOD lines. API half passes; UI half fails until FE blacklist removed.

Automation Notes: Assert by socCode, not display name. Backend gating is PlanFilter.ExcludePlans on !DeviceState.IsByod — assert API response independently of UI. Keep FE-blacklist check as explicit pre-assertion so failure message is diagnostic. Live 2026-07-09: BYOD line got 9 plans incl. both — backend correct; UI blocked by FE bug.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): BYOD IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI. A-Stock IMEI (negative control) = catalog A-Stock IMEI from data sheet. SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM.

steps.action: Rep takes a BYOD line to plan selection
steps.expected: [BE] selection-options/plans includes socCodes 40L1WEB and BYOD10GB3; [FE] both rendered in the plan grid (gated by FE blacklist bug)

steps.action: Rep selects 40L1WEB
steps.expected: [FE] plan option selected (coupon requirement -> BYOD-015)

steps.action: Rep re-selects BYOD10GB3
steps.expected: [FE] plan option selected

steps.action: (Negative) Rep takes an A-Stock line to plan selection
steps.expected: [Both] neither socCode in the API response NOR in the rendered grid

test_cases.name: BYOD-015 | Plan 40L1WEB Requires MMP Coupon COUP_NEU3F3
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-5 (passed live 2026-07-09) | Type: Negative + Positive | Technique: Decision table (plan x coupon presence).

Feature: 40L1WEB plan may only be sold together with its MMP coupon COUP_NEU3F3. RTGO must block finishing the line on 40L1WEB unless that coupon is selected — rep cannot skip promotions or pick other promos and move on. BYOD10GB3 has no such requirement. Mirrors legacy RT POS rule.

Decision table: R1 40L1WEB + no (skip promotions) -> blocked. R2 40L1WEB + other promos only -> rejected with message. R3 40L1WEB + COUP_NEU3F3 -> proceeds. R4 BYOD10GB3 + no coupon -> proceeds.

Related: RTPL-2454 (Closed/Done; product-confirmed), RTPL-2438.

Automation Notes: Pairing is config-driven (plan->coupon), not hardcoded; other plans unaffected. Assert cart discount equals backend value (RTPL-2575 showed -$44 FE display drift on BYOD-IPHONE + 40L1WEB). Passed live 2026-07-09: exact required-promotion-missing message + requiredPromotionCodes:["COUP_NEU3F3"].

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI. SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM. Plans/coupon = 40L1WEB, BYOD10GB3; coupon COUP_NEU3F3 (rep picks from offers, not auto-added).

steps.action: (R1) Rep selects 40L1WEB, then tries to Skip promotions
steps.expected: [Both] 400 required-promotion-missing; message "Please select the promo coupon or choose another rate plan."; flow stays at promotions

steps.action: (R2) Rep selects a different promo (not COUP_NEU3F3) and continues
steps.expected: [Both] rejected with the same message; flow stays

steps.action: (R3) Rep selects COUP_NEU3F3 and continues
steps.expected: [Both] accepted; flow advances to add-ons

steps.action: (R4) Rep selects BYOD10GB3 and skips promotions
steps.expected: [FE] no block; flow advances

test_cases.name: BYOD-016 | Receipt / Order Summary Accuracy — Plan Price Not $0
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (Order Summary passed live; add printed-receipt render assert) | Type: Positive | Technique: Output-based testing across price partitions.

Feature: Order Summary and printed receipt must show plan's real monthly charge — early bug rendered $0 for plan on BYOD sales only; another showed wrong cart discount. Proves money customer sees is correct across several plan price points, consistent between cart and backend.

Related: RTPL-2572 (receipt $0 plan charge), RTPL-2575 (cart -$44 display drift).

Automation Notes: Assert receipt DOM/HTML price equals plan catalog value from API in same run — do not pin literal price. Live 2026-07-09: Order Summary totalMrc 36 = plan 30 + protection 6 (plan not $0) — passed; printed-receipt render still to be added for AR-5.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI (also an iPhone-TAC run for RTPL-2575 overlap). SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM. Plans (price partitions) = BYOD10GB3 (~$30), BFLX50 (~$50), BFLX60 (~$60).

steps.action: Rep completes a BYOD activation on BYOD10GB3 to Order Summary
steps.expected: [Both] Order Summary plan monthly = ~$30.00 (NOT $0.00); device $0.00; SIM per BYOD-011

steps.action: Rep reviews all Order Summary line items
steps.expected: [FE] fields present: Order ID, Account Number, Lines in order, Total MRC, Total tax & fees, due immediately

steps.action: Rep pays cash and opens the printed receipt
steps.expected: [Both] receipt plan price = plan catalog value fetched from selection-options in the same run (self-consistent oracle); per-line detail shows Device Type + IMEI

steps.action: Repeat across the BFLX50 and BFLX60 partitions (data-driven)
steps.expected: [Both] no hardcoded $0 anywhere; cart discount equals backend value at every step (no FE recompute drift)

test_cases.name: BYOD-017 | Protection Step for BYOD — Offers Load, BYOD SOC, Skip Path
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (needs no-IMEI2 BYOD variant provisioned) | Type: Positive + Negative | Technique: EP (add vs skip) + config partition (BYOD SOC tier).

Feature: At protection step rep can add device insurance or decline. For BYOD line, offers must load without Metro inventory error, must show BYOD-specific insurance SOCs (not A-Stock tiers), skip path must work — past bug threw Metro error on BYOD lines with no IMEI2 then returned empty plan list afterward.

Related: RTPL-2433 (fetch-offers error 990007371 + null plans afterward).

Automation Notes: Run explicitly with no-IMEI2 BYOD variant — RTPL-2433's trigger. Chain plans-not-null assertion right after protection — catches cascading failure. Live 2026-07-09 (add + skip): offers loaded (MCPY/MAPY/MBPY), no 990007371, skip clean, plans non-null.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI — run the no-IMEI2 variant (RTPL-2433 trigger). SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM. Protection = first offered tier (add run); none (skip run).

steps.action: Rep reaches the protection step and fetches offers
steps.expected: [Both] offers load; no error toast/modal; no Metro error 990007371

steps.action: Rep reads the offered insurance SOC
steps.expected: [BE] BYOD-tier SOC (NWBY1 $6 / NYBY1 $7 by state), not A-Stock PHP tiers

steps.action: (Add run) Rep selects a protection offer and continues
steps.expected: [Both] Order Summary gains a protection line; [BE] protectionConfigDetails populated

steps.action: (Skip run) Rep clicks Skip and confirms "Decline all protection options?"
steps.expected: [Both] protection declined; [BE] protectionConfigDetails.skipped=true, no error

steps.action: After the protection step, rep reaches plan selection
steps.expected: [BE] selection-options/plans non-null (RTPL-2433 cascading symptom: plans returned null after the error)

test_cases.name: BYOD-018 | Multi-Line BYOD — Phone + Watch Invoice Integrity
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-2 (needs real watch IMEI + EID; blocked with WEAR-001/EID data) | Type: Positive | Technique: EP (multi-line composition) + output completeness oracle.

Feature: Customer can activate phone + paired watch on one order. Both lines must be itemized on invoice and in persisted SaleRecord — earlier bug silently dropped watch line from SaleRecord though full payment taken, causing reconciliation failures. Proves nothing gets dropped. Watch is line 2 — watch cannot be line 1.

Related: RTPL-2641 (watch line dropped from SaleRecord — Verified).

Automation Notes: Decisive oracle = SaleRecord item COUNT (must be 2), not invoice rendering. Assert cart "Device" row per line BEFORE payment — cheap early signal. BYOD line resolving to ZERO sale items must fail loudly/alert, never persist silently (RTPL-2641 AC). Blocked with WEAR-001 (watch classification) + corrupt EID data — raise once real watch IMEI/EID provisioned.

Preconditions / Test Data:
ConfigCat enable_subscriber_activation_byod = ON and enable_subscriber_activation_watch = ON. Line 1 is a BYOD phone; watch added as line 2 via add-line (rule 9).

Test Data (FE): Line 1 IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI. Line 1 SIM = first unused physical SIM (or eSIM EID). Line 2 IMEI = real watch IMEI (Apple, then Samsung BYOW-SAM for pairwise). Line 2 EID = valid 32-digit EID. Plans (phone + paired watch) and pairing MDN discovered at run time.

steps.action: Rep activates line 1 (BYOD phone) through activate-subscriber
steps.expected: [BE] phone line activated

steps.action: Rep clicks Add a line and enters the watch IMEI -> accept BYOD -> BYOW type -> EID -> paired plan + pairing MDN -> activate
steps.expected: [BE] watch line activated; [Both] watch line's cart breakdown HAS a "Device" row at $0.00 (missing Device row is the drop precursor)

steps.action: Rep completes the order and reviews Order Summary
steps.expected: [Both] "Lines in order: 2"; both a LINE ONLY ACTIVATION

steps.action: Rep completes Add Accessory -> cash payment (exact)
steps.expected: [BE] SaleRecord contains EXACTLY 2 LINE ONLY ACTIVATION items + payment; watch LineState.Pricing.Device non-null; QPAY total matches Order Summary

steps.action: Repeat with a Samsung watch (BYOW-SAM) as line 2 (pairwise)
steps.expected: [BE] same integrity

test_cases.name: BYOD-019 | BYOD Child Payment SaleRecord — No ItemId=0 Failure
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-5 (passed live 2026-07-09) | Type: Positive (regression) | Technique: EP on ItemId partitions (null / 0 / >0).

Feature: BYOD device has no physical inventory item, so device item id is 0. Payment/sale-record builder must handle that — earlier bug treated 0 as invalid (422 "ItemId must be greater than 0"): subscriber activated, money collected, but SaleRecord never saved — orphan sale retried forever. Proves BYOD sale persists cleanly.

Related: RTPL-2436 (Closed/Done), RTPL-1967 (regression origin).

Automation Notes: End-of-suite check: scan for TEMP-/orphan sales after every BYOD run (shared oracle with BYOD-013). Regression weight: 6 of 7 failing QA payment processes were BYOD — any recurrence is Critical. Passed live 2026-07-09: SaleRecord 40286 created first attempt, sku:"BYOD" device $0, no ItemId=0 error.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI (device resolves to sku:"BYOD", itemId 0). SIM (ICCID) = first unused physical SIM.

steps.action: Rep completes a BYOD activation through cash payment (first eligible values, cash exact)
steps.expected: [BE] payment succeeds AND SaleRecord created in the same run — no 422 ItemId must be greater than 0

steps.action: Inspect the sale items
steps.expected: [BE] no device sale-item with ItemId=0; SIM/plan lines present

steps.action: Check the retry queue for this sale
steps.expected: [BE] zero deterministic-failure retries; no orphan/TEMP- sale

test_cases.name: BYOD-020 | Feature Flag OFF — BYOD Path Gated
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (needs ConfigCat flag-toggle fixture) | Type: Negative | Technique: Configuration testing (flag state transition ON->OFF->ON).

Feature: BYOD path gated by feature flag. When enable_subscriber_activation_byod OFF, BYOD-eligible IMEI must NOT open BYOD flow — rep sees "Device not supported, use RTPOS" dialog. Prevents half-built BYOD leaking to stores before release. Toggling back ON must restore flow.

Related: RTPL-2205 (flag constant EnableByod), device-support widget.

Automation Notes: Wrap flag toggle in fixture with ~3s propagation wait and GUARANTEED teardown reset. Pair with positive smoke (BYOD-001) immediately after re-enabling.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI = BYOD-eligible customer-owned IMEI (random Luhn). Flag = enable_subscriber_activation_byod toggled OFF then ON (ConfigCat).

steps.action: Set the flag OFF and wait for propagation
steps.expected: [BE] flag confirmed OFF

steps.action: Rep enters the BYOD-eligible IMEI
steps.expected: [Both] error unsupported-device-type; "Device not supported" dialog — subtitle "The IMEI # {0} you entered can't be activated in RTGO at this time.", footer "Please use RTPOS to complete this activation."; single Back button

steps.action: Rep confirms no BYOD entry exists
steps.expected: [Both] no "IMEI not found" drawer; no accept-add-as-byod; [BE] no CreateByodDevice, no line advances past validate-device

steps.action: Set the flag ON and re-enter the IMEI
steps.expected: [FE] BYOD "IMEI not found" drawer appears (as BYOD-002) — toggle works both ways

test_cases.name: BYOD-021 | Decline BYOD — Terminal Cancel Path
test_cases.custom_requirements:
AR: AR-4 (strong API oracle) | Type: Negative | Technique: State transition (invalid-continuation guard).

Feature: If rep decides not to register customer-owned device, declining BYOD offer must cleanly cancel that line — any later activity call on cancelled line must be rejected, not silently accepted and blow up later. Guards against zombie lines lingering in an order.

Related: RTPL-2069 (AC-4), backend-mock UC-CDI-7 (validation-aborted).

Automation Notes: Mirrors mock contract UC-CDI-7 at UI/API level. Guard against RTPL-2418-style "200 OK now, explode later" on terminal states.

Preconditions / Test Data:
Test Data (FE): IMEI = random Luhn-valid BYOD handset IMEI.

steps.action: Rep reaches the BYOD drawer and clicks Back
steps.expected: [Both] decline-add-as-byod/cancel-line dispatched; line cancelled; rep returned to IMEI entry

steps.action: (API) Attempt a further activity on the cancelled line (POST collect-device-info)
steps.expected: [BE] HTTP 400 validation-aborted — no zombie continuation

steps.action: Rep starts a fresh line with a corrected IMEI
steps.expected: [Both] works normally; [BE] cancelled line emits no Metro calls and is excluded from the order line count
